concerns.text: Tasks T013-T018 implement synthetic data generation and simulation only. The spec (FR-001, FR-006) and User Story 1 explicitly mandate ingesting 'actual VR interaction data' and 'raw data from OSF/HuggingFace'. The tasks silently relax this requirement to 'simulation' without a corresponding task to ingest real data or a formal scope change in the spec, violating the constraint that tasks must not weaken FRs.

Task T025 validates parameter recovery against 'ground_truth_effect' (simulation). The spec (SC-002) requires measuring model improvement (ΔAIC > 10) against a baseline to determine 'strong evidence'. The tasks replace the scientific evidence requirement with a validation-only check, effectively dropping the acceptance criterion for the hypothesis test defined in the spec.

Task T032 states the report must 'defer scientific claims to Phase 4'. While the plan acknowledges this, the spec (US-3) requires the report to 'include a clear statement of the findings... and whether the evidence supports the hypothesis'. The task explicitly narrows the deliverable to a 'Pipeline Validation' status, dropping the requirement to report on the hypothesis itself, which is a silent weakening of the spec's acceptance criteria.

Task T030 description lists the sensitivity analysis thresholds as '{, 10, 20}', missing the value '2'. This is a typo that contradicts the spec (FR-005, SC-004) which explicitly requires the set {2, 10, 20}. If implemented literally, the task fails to satisfy the acceptance criterion.
